prompt: All of the following correctly describe an imaging transducer except:
Shorter duration events (such as dampened pulses) are more likely to have wide bandwidth.
The acoustic impedance of the matching layer is approximately the same as that of the skin.
Imaging transducers are usually of wide rather than narrow bandwidth.
A very high Q factor transducer is used more often in diagnostic imaging than a low Q factor.
A pulse with a long pulse duration is likely to have a narrow bandwidth.
The damping material in a transducer increases the sensitivity.
The damping material in a transducer increases the pulse length.
The damping material in a transducer decreases the pulse duration.
The damping material in a transducer improves the system's lateral resolution.
The damping material in a transducer improves the system's longitudinal resolution.
The dampening material in a transducer decreases bandwidth.
The damping material in a transducer decreases the quality factor.
If the frequency of the electrical excitation voltage of a pulsed wave transducer is 6 MHz, then the operating frequency of the transducer is 6 MHz.
If the PRF of a transducer is increased, then the frequency of the sound produced by the transducer remains the same.
The diameter of the active element of a transducer helps determine the frequency of the sound produced by the transducer.
Two PZT crystals are made from the same material. The thicker crystal will make a pulsed transducer with a higher frequency.
Two PZT crystals are made from the same material. The thicker crystal will make a continuous wave transducer with a lower frequency.
The normal propagation speed in PZT material is about 3-5 times greater than in soft tissue.
The impedance of a transducer active element is 1,900,000 Rayls, and the impedance of the skin is 1,400,000 Rayls. What is an acceptable impedance for the matching layer?
Which of the following crystals will produce sound with the lowest frequency?
choices: thin and with a low speed
thin and with a high speed
thick and with a low speed
thick and with a high speed
